questions.question: A truck is loaded with $2/3$ cement and $1/3$ sand. If $300$ kg of sand is added, what fraction of the mixture is now sand?
A shipping company has $480$ boxes of fragile items and $720$ boxes of non-fragile items. What is the ratio of fragile to non-fragile items?
A train has $30$ cars, each with $20%$ of its capacity filled with fuel. If $5$ more cars are added, each with $30%$ capacity filled, what percentage of the total capacity is now filled?
A cargo ship travels $360$ km in $6$ hours with a $20%$ cargo load. How far will it travel in $4$ hours with a $30%$ cargo load, assuming the speed remains constant?

A traffic flow model estimates that $30%$ of the cars on the road are traveling at $60$ km/h. If $20%$ are traveling at $80$ km/h, what percentage of the cars are traveling at speeds other than $60$ km/h or $80$ km/h?

A company has two types of delivery trucks, $A$ and $B$. Truck $A$ travels at $50$ km/h and truck $B$ travels at $75$ km/h. If $60%$ of the deliveries are made by truck $A$ and $40%$ by truck $B$, what is the average speed of all deliveries?
A logistics company has a $25%$ margin on its shipping services. If it reduces its prices by $15%$, what is the new margin as a percentage of the original price?

A transportation company has two routes, $X$ and $Y$, with travel times of $2$ hours and $3$ hours respectively. If $40%$ of the passengers use route $X$ and $60%$ use route $Y$, what is the average travel time for all passengers?

A shipping company has two types of containers, small and large. The small containers have a $20%$ chance of being damaged during shipping, while the large containers have a $10%$ chance. If $40%$ of the containers are small and $60%$ are large, what is the overall probability that a container will be damaged during shipping? Show your work and calculate the answer.

A train has $20$ cars, each with a $25%$ chance of being filled with cargo. If $5$ more cars are added, each with a $30%$ chance of being filled, what is the probability that exactly $12$ cars are filled with cargo? Use the binomial probability formula to calculate the answer. Show your work and provide the final probability.

teacher_rules: Read the questions carefully before answering
Use the space provided to show your work
